factors.summary: Demand is accelerating across all service lines.

evidence.text: Revenues for the year rose 5.2 percent to $1.485 billion compared to $1.412 billion for the prior year. All of our business lines experienced growth for the year, with residential pest control revenues up 6.6%, commercial pest control revenues up 3.3% and termite revenues up 4.6%.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The summary indicates accelerating demand across all service lines, supported by evidence of revenue growth in each segment.

factors.summary: The company has minimal pricing power, with less than 2% of revenue increase coming from pricing actions.

evidence.text: The Company implemented its traditional price increase program in June 2015. Less than 2% of the Company’s revenue increase came from pricing actions.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Minimal pricing power is evident, as only less than 2% of revenue growth came from pricing actions.

factors.summary: Revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments.

evidence.text: The Company’s gross margin increased to 50.4% for 2015 compared to 49.9% in 2014. Sales, general and administrative expense decreased in 2015 to 31.2% of revenue versus 31.3% in 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The revenue mix is shifting towards higher-margin segments, as evidenced by increased gross margins.

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, contributing 4.2% of revenue increase.

evidence.text: Organic growth and pricing accounted for approximately 4.2% of our increase and our acquisitions contributed the remaining revenue growth.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Organic revenue growth is accelerating, contributing significantly to revenue increases.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across all service lines.

evidence.text: All of our business lines experienced growth for the year, with residential pest control revenues up 6.6%, commercial pest control revenues up 3.3% and termite revenues up 4.6%.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Unit volumes are growing across all service lines, supported by evidence of revenue increases in each segment.

factors.summary: Residential and commercial pest control, as well as termite services, are growing fastest.

evidence.text: Commercial pest control represented approximately 41% of the Company’s revenue in 2015 and grew 3.3% in 2015 due to increases in sales, improvements in cancellations, increased bed bug revenue, an increase in commercial fumigations, and acquisitions. Residential pest control which also represented approximately 41% of the Company’s revenue, increased 6.6% driven by increased leads, closures and pricing as well as increased TAEXX® homebuilder installations, bed bug revenues and acquisitions. The Company’s termite business, which represented approximately 17% of the Company’s revenue, grew 4.6% in 2015 due to increases in drywood fumigations and ancillary service sales, and acquisitions.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Residential and commercial pest control, as well as termite services, are growing fastest, indicating strong market momentum.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable with reduced fleet costs due to lower gasoline prices.

evidence.text: Gross margin for the year increased to 50.4% for 2015 compared to 49.9% for 2014 due to reduced fleet costs due to the drop in gasoline prices, favorable service salaries as we continue to improve our routing and scheduling to maximize efficiencies and favorable administrative salary margins as we continue to focus on efficiency.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Input costs are stable with reduced fleet costs due to lower gasoline prices, which is favorable for margins.

factors.summary: Labor costs are increasing due to higher group premiums.

evidence.text: The favorable margins were partially offset by our casualty claim development, increases in supplies as a result of increased sales and increases in personnel related costs as group premiums continue to increase.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Labor costs are increasing due to higher group premiums, which negatively impacts margins.

factors.summary: Gross and operating margins are improving due to cost controls and pricing.

evidence.text: The Company’s gross margin increased to 50.4% for 2015 compared to 49.9% in 2014. Sales, general and administrative expense decreased in 2015 to 31.2% of revenue versus 31.3% in 2014. The Company’s depreciation and amortization margin decreased 0.1 point to 3.0 % in 2015 compared to 3.1% in 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improvement in gross and operating margins indicates positive developments.

factors.summary: Cost actions are delivering measurable savings through improved routing and scheduling.

evidence.text: Gross margin for the year increased to 50.4% for 2015 compared to 49.9% for 2014 due to reduced fleet costs due to the drop in gasoline prices, favorable service salaries as we continue to improve our routing and scheduling to maximize efficiencies and favorable administrative salary margins as we continue to focus on efficiency.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Measurable savings from routing and scheduling improvements suggest positive progress.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving through better routing and scheduling.

evidence.text: Gross margin for the year increased to 50.4% for 2015 compared to 49.9% for 2014 due to reduced fleet costs due to the drop in gasoline prices, favorable service salaries as we continue to improve our routing and scheduling to maximize efficiencies and favorable administrative salary margins as we continue to focus on efficiency.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Improved productivity through routing and scheduling supports positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Capacity utilization is increasing through technology investments and expansion.

evidence.text: The Company is pleased with the success we had with the rollout of our CRM system (BOSS) during the year. We ended 2015 with 50 percent of our Orkin branches on the system. We currently expect to have all Orkin locations on BOSS by the end of the third quarter of this year. Orkin recently announced that we extended our presence in North America, South America, Europe, the Middle East and Asia, with the addition of nine new franchises.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Technology investments and expansion indicate positive developments in utilization.

factors.summary: Management is confident with investments in CRM systems and international expansion.

evidence.text: We are pleased with the success we had with the rollout of our CRM system (BOSS) during the year. We ended 2015 with 50 percent of our Orkin branches on the system. We currently expect to have all Orkin locations on BOSS by the end of the third quarter of this year. Orkin recently announced that we extended our presence in North America, South America, Europe, the Middle East and Asia, with the addition of nine new franchises.

sentiment.label: very_positive
sentiment.rationale: Confidence in CRM systems and international expansion reflects very positive sentiment.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include unfavorable exchange rates and competition.

evidence.text: The Company’s foreign operations accounted for approximately 7% and 8% of total revenues for the years ended December 31, 2015 and 2014, respectively. Currency exchange translation is the cause of the decreased percentage. The Company established new franchises in Mexico, Colombia, Republic of Georgia, Qatar, China, and South Korea in 2015 for a total of 48 international franchises at December 31, 2015 with 37 at December 31, 2014.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Unfavorable exchange rates and competition pose significant risks.

factors.summary: Long-term strategy is focused on strategic acquisitions and international expansion.

evidence.text: Strategic acquisitions remain a priority for Rollins, and as in the past, we will continue to seek out companies that are a 'fit' for us in both, the pest control and wildlife areas of our business. The Company established new franchises in Mexico, Colombia, Republic of Georgia, Qatar, China, and South Korea in 2015 for a total of 48 international franchises at December 31, 2015 with 37 at December 31, 2014.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Focus on acquisitions and international expansion indicates a positive long-term strategy.

factors.summary: Key sensitivities include currency exchange and competition.

evidence.text: The Company’s foreign operations accounted for approximately 7% and 8% of total revenues for the years ended December 31, 2015 and 2014, respectively. Currency exchange translation is the cause of the decreased percentage. The Company established new franchises in Mexico, Colombia, Republic of Georgia, Qatar, China, and South Korea in 2015 for a total of 48 international franchises at December 31, 2015 with 37 at December 31, 2014.

sentiment.label: negative
sentiment.rationale: Currency exchange and competition are key sensitivities, indicating negative sentiment.

factors.summary: Digital automation is improving through the rollout of the BOSS CRM system.

evidence.text: We are pleased with the success we had with the rollout of our CRM system (BOSS) during the year. We ended 2015 with 50 percent of our Orkin branches on the system. We currently expect to have all Orkin locations on BOSS by the end of the third quarter of this year.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: The successful rollout of the BOSS CRM system indicates improvement in digital automation.

factors.summary: The company is gaining market share through acquisitions and new franchises.

evidence.text: The Company established new franchises in Mexico, Colombia, Republic of Georgia, Qatar, China, and South Korea in 2015 for a total of 48 international franchises at December 31, 2015 with 37 at December 31, 2014. Orkin had 99 and 92 franchises (domestic and international) at December 31, 2015 and 2014, respectively.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Growth in international franchises suggests an increase in market share.

factors.summary: The competitive environment is stable with some expansion.

evidence.text: Strategic acquisitions remain a priority for Rollins, and as in the past, we will continue to seek out companies that are a 'fit' for us in both, the pest control and wildlife areas of our business.

sentiment.label: neutral
sentiment.rationale: The competitive environment is described as stable with some expansion, indicating no clear trend.

factors.summary: Shareholder returns are increasing with higher dividends and share repurchases.

evidence.text: The Company used cash of $97.2 million on financing activities for the year ended December 31, 2015, compared to $106.5 million and $75.7 million during 2014 and 2013, respectively. A total of $91.8 million was paid in cash dividends ($0.42 per share) during the year ended December 31, 2015 including a special dividend paid in December 2015 of $0.10 per share, compared to $75.8 million paid in cash dividends ($0.35 per share) during the year ended December 31, 2014, including a special dividend paid in December 2014 of $0.07 per share and $65.7 million ($0.30 per share) during the year ended December 31, 2013, including a special dividend paid in December 2013 of $0.06 per share.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Higher dividends and share repurchases indicate increased shareholder returns.

factors.summary: Free cash flow generation is improving with strong operating cash flows.

evidence.text: The Company’s operations generated cash of $196.4 million for the year ended December 31, 2015 primarily from net income of $152.1 million, compared with cash provided by operating activities of $194.1 million in 2014 and $162.7 million in 2013.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Strong operating cash flows show improvement in free cash flow generation.

factors.summary: Acquisitions are contributing to revenue growth and creating value by expanding capabilities.

evidence.text: Organic growth and pricing accounted for approximately 4.2% of our increase and our acquisitions contributed the remaining revenue growth. The Company made contributions totaling $5.0 million to the Rollins, Inc. and its wholly-owned subsidiaries’ defined benefit retirement plans (the “Plans”) during the year ended December 31, 2015 and $5.3 million and $5.0 million during the years ended December 31, 2014 and 2013, respectively, as a result of the Plans’ funding status.

The Company made contributions totaling $5.0 million to the Rollins, Inc. and its wholly-owned subsidiaries’ defined benefit retirement plans (the “Plans”) during the year ended December 31, 2015 and $5.3 million and $5.0 million during the years ended December 31, 2014 and 2013, respectively, as a result of the Plans’ funding status.

sentiment.label: positive
sentiment.rationale: Acquisitions are contributing to revenue growth, indicating positive activity.
